8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))

84	if __name__ == "__main__":

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

## Complete Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
bookmark-cli/
├── pyproject.toml          (Project configuration)

│   └── formatter.py        (Output formatting - table and JSON)

    ├── __init__.py         (Empty package marker)
    ├── test_storage.py     (Storage operations tests)
    └── test_search.py      (Search functionality tests - EXPECTS search.py TO BE CREATED)
```

### 2. CONFIGURATION FILES

**pyproject.toml** (21 lines):
- Build system: setuptools with wheel
- Python requirement: >=3.10
- Package name: bookmark-cli, version 1.0.0
- Test dependency: pytest>=7.4.0
- CLI entry point: `bookmark = "src.cli:main"`
- Test paths configured: `["tests"]`

### 3. DATA MODEL (src/models.py)

**Bookmark dataclass** with fields:
- `id: str` - UUID identifier
- `url: str` - URL to bookmark
- `title: str` - Display title
- `tags: List[str]` - List of tags (default: empty list)
- `notes: str` - Additional notes (default: "")
- `created_at: str` - ISO format timestamp (auto-generated if not provided)

Key methods:
- `to_dict()` - Serializes bookmark to dictionary
- `from_dict(data)` - Class method to deserialize from dictionary

### 4. STORAGE LAYER (src/storage.py)

**Default storage location**: `~/.bookmarks.json` (JSON file format)

**Core functions**:
- `load_bookmarks(db_path)` - Loads all bookmarks from JSON file, returns empty list if file doesn't exist
- `save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)` - Saves bookmarks to JSON file with 2-space indentation
- `add_bookmark(url, title, tags, notes, db_path)` - Creates new bookmark with UUID, appends to storage, returns created bookmark
- `delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` - Removes bookmark by ID, returns boolean
- `get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` - Retrieves single bookmark by ID, returns None if not found

All functions accept optional `db_path` parameter for testing purposes.

### 5. OUTPUT FORMATTING (src/formatter.py)

**format_table(bookmarks) -> str**:
- Creates fixed-width columnar table output
- Column widths: ID (8), Title (30), URL (40), Tags (20)
- Shows short ID (first 8 chars of UUID)
- Truncates long values to fit column widths
- Returns "No bookmarks found." if list is empty
- Displays header, separator line, and data rows

**format_json(bookmarks) -> str**:
- Converts bookmarks list to JSON array
- Uses 2-space indentation
- Returns raw JSON string

### 6. CLI FRAMEWORK & COMMAND STRUCTURE (src/cli.py)

**Framework**: Python's built-in `argparse`

**Main components**:
- `build_parser()` - Creates ArgumentParser with subcommands
- `main(argv=None)` - Entry point that parses args and dispatches to command handlers

**Existing commands**:

1. **add** - Add a new bookmark
   - Positional args: `url`, `title`
   - Optional args: `--tags` (nargs="*", default=[]), `--notes` (default="")
   - Output: "Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}"

2. **list** - List all bookmarks
   - Optional: `--format` (choices: "table", "json", default: "table")
   - Output: Formatted table or JSON

3. **delete** - Delete a bookmark
   - Positional arg: `id` (bookmark ID)
   - Output: Success/failure message, exits with code 1 on failure

4. **show** - Show single bookmark
   - Positional arg: `id` (bookmark ID)
   - Output: Formatted as table, exits with code 1 if not found

**Command dispatcher pattern**:
- Uses `if args.command == "command_name":` chain
- Each command calls appropriate storage/formatter functions
- Error messages printed to stdout

### 7. TEST FILES

**test_storage.py** (117 lines):
- Tests all storage functions with temporary file fixtures
- Validates JSON persistence
- Tests edge cases: missing files, empty results, duplicate IDs

**test_search.py** (180 lines):
- **CRITICAL**: This file imports from `src.search` which does NOT YET exist
- Expects three search functions to be implemented: `search()`, `search_by_title()`, `search_by_tag()`, `search_by_domain()`
- Fixture: 5 sample bookmarks with Python/JS/web dev themes

**Expected search module behavior** (from tests):

1. **search_by_title(bookmarks, query: str) -> List[Bookmark]**
   - Case-insensitive substring matching
   - Returns empty list if no matches

2. **search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]**
   - Exact tag matching (case-sensitive based on tests)
   - Returns empty list if no matches

3. **search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]**
   - Extracts domain from URL and matches
   - Matches both full domain and subdomains
   - Example: "github.com" matches "https://github.com/*" URLs

4. **search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]**
   - Combined search with AND logic (all criteria must match)
   - If no criteria provided, returns all bookmarks
   - Returns empty list if no bookmarks match all criteria

**CLI integration tests** (from test_search.py):
- Expects `search` subcommand to be added to parser
- Arguments: `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain`, `--format` (json or table, default table)
- Test assertions verify parser accepts these arguments

### 8. IMPLEMENTATION PATTERNS TO FOLLOW

**Command addition pattern**:
1. Add subparser in `build_parser()` with command name and help text
2. Add positional/optional arguments to subparser
3. Add condition in `main()` dispatcher: `elif args.command == "new_command":`
4. Call storage/utility functions and format output
5. Use `sys.exit(1)` for error conditions

**Function naming**: Simple, descriptive, module-level functions (not classes for commands)

**Testing pattern**: Use pytest with fixtures for temporary db paths and sample data

**Error handling**: Print error message and exit with code 1 on failures

### 9. CRITICAL FINDINGS FOR SEARCH IMPLEMENTATION

The codebase is ready for the search feature. The test file (`test_search.py`) already defines:
- Exact function signatures needed
- Expected behavior with comprehensive test cases
- CLI integration requirements
- Format output options (table and JSON)

**Missing**: Only `src/search.py` module and CLI integration in `src/cli.py` are needed.

All required imports, models, storage functions, and formatters are already in place and properly structured.

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context
The bookmark CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. The test suite already includes `tests/test_search.py` which imports from `src.search` (a module that does not yet exist) and verifies exact function signatures, behavior, and CLI integration. The goal is to implement `src/search.py` and wire the `search` subcommand into `src/cli.py` so all tests pass.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `src/search.py` | **Create** — four search functions |
| `src/cli.py` | **Modify** — add subparser + dispatcher for `search` |

---

## Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Implement four module-level functions. Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` (stdlib) for domain extraction.

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains `query` (case-insensitive substring)."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have `tag` as an exact match in their tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL netloc exactly equals `domain`."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Filter bookmarks with AND logic across all provided criteria.
    
    If no criteria are given, returns all bookmarks unchanged.
    """
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
```

**Design notes:**
- `search_by_title`: lowercase both sides — tests pass `"python"` and expect matches on `"Python Tutorial"` and `"RunPod Python SDK"`.
- `search_by_tag`: plain `in` membership — tags are strings, tests expect exact match (`"flask"` → 1 result, `"docs"` → 3).
- `search_by_domain`: `urlparse(url).netloc` gives `"github.com"` from `"https://github.com/..."` and `"docs.python.org"` from `"https://docs.python.org/..."` — simple equality covers all test cases.
- `search`: applies filters sequentially (AND logic); if no filter given, the list passes through each unchanged, returning all bookmarks.

---

## Step 2 — Update `src/cli.py`

### 2a. Add import at the top
Add `search` import alongside existing imports:

```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b. Add `search` subparser inside `build_parser()`
Add after the `show` subparser block:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag",   default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain",default=None, help="Filter by domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

### 2c. Add dispatcher branch inside `main()`
Add after the `elif args.command == "show":` block:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

**Design notes:**
- Follows the exact same pattern as `list` — load all bookmarks, apply search filter, format and print.
- Uses existing `format_table` / `format_json` from `src/formatter.py` (already imported in `cli.py`).
- All three filter args default to `None`; passing them through to `search()` means missing args are ignored (AND logic only applies active filters).
- No `sys.exit(1)` needed — returning zero results is not an error (prints "No bookmarks found." via `format_table`).

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite:

```bash
pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected: all tests in `test_storage.py` continue to pass, all tests in `test_search.py` now pass:
- `TestSearchByTitle` (3 tests)
- `TestSearchByTag` (3 tests)
- `TestSearchByDomain` (3 tests)
- `TestSearchCombined` (7 tests)
- `TestSearchCLI` (2 tests)

Smoke-test the CLI manually:
```bash
bookmark add https://github.com/pallets/flask "Flask" --tags python flask web
bookmark add https://docs.python.org/3/ "Python Docs" --tags python docs
bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag flask
bookmark search --domain github.com
bookmark search --title python --tag docs
bookmark search --title python --format json
```

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )
